content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a board game, and is currently at position XXX.

To win, he needs to reach position 505050.

Chef has a pair of 666-sided dice, which will decide how he moves - he will roll the pair of dice, sum up the numbers on them, and then move exactly that many steps forward.

More precisely, if the dice show values of d1d_1d1​ and d2d_2d2​, Chef must move from XXX to (X+d1+d2)(X+d_1+d_2)(X+d1​+d2​).

However, Chef's dice are a bit weird: rather than starting from 111, they start from YYY.

That is, the values on a single die are Y,Y+1,Y+2,Y+3,Y+4,Y+5Y, Y+1, Y+2, Y+3, Y+4, Y+5Y,Y+1,Y+2,Y+3,Y+4,Y+5.

Is it possible for Chef to win the game by reaching position 505050, after **exactly one turn**?

Note that Chef must exactly land on position 505050 after his move - going beyond it does not count as a win.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line and only of each test case contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the current position of Chef and the starting value on the dice, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: Yes\text{Yes}Yes if Chef can reach position 505050 in exactly one turn, and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e. the strings NO,No,nO,\text{NO}, \text{No}, \text{nO},NO,No,nO, and no\text{no}no will all be treated as equivalent.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef is at X=46X = 46X=46 and needs to reach 505050. The dice have the values 111 to 666 on them.
This is possible: one way is if the dice roll values (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), for example.
Test case 222: Chef is at X=36X = 36X=36 and needs to reach 505050. The dice have the values 111 to 666 on them.
This is impossible: no matter what the dice show, reaching 505050 is not possible.
Test case 333: Chef is at X=36X = 36X=36 and needs to reach 505050. The dice have the values 222 to 777 on them.
This is possible if the dice roll values (7,7)(7, 7)(7,7).
